concerns.text: Data pairing assumption: Spec assumes transcriptomics (GEO) and metabolomics (Metabolomics Workbench) data can be 'aligned by experimental condition ID' from the SAME biological samples. Public omics repositories rarely contain paired multi-omics data from identical samples. Different studies with the same condition annotation are NOT the same samples. This makes the core research design potentially unimplementable as specified.

FR-002 requires 'matched targeted metabolomics data...for the same experimental condition IDs' but does not verify that GEO and Metabolomics Workbench studies share actual sample-level pairing. Without true sample-level pairing, the predictive model trains on mismatched data, invalidating all downstream results.

Cross-species modeling (Arabidopsis + Solanum) without species-specific normalization or batch correction. Gene expression scales and pathway annotations differ between species. Training a single Ridge model across species without accounting for this confounding may produce spurious correlations.

Assumptions state empirical claims as facts (e.g., 'Metabolomics Workbench contains targeted quantification...in ≥80% of plant defense studies', 'KEGG pathway annotations...cover ≥75% of known genes'). These should be marked as [deferred] or sourced with citations per Constitution Principle II.
